name: Charles Trenet
abstract: Louis Charles Augustin Georges Trenet (French pronunciation: [lwi ʃaʁl oɡystɛ̃ ʒɔʁʒ tʁenɛ] ; 18 May 1913 – 19 February 2001) was a renowned French singer-songwriter who composed both the music and the lyrics for nearly 1,000 songs over a career that lasted more than 60 years. These songs include "Boum!" (1938), "La Mer" (1946) and "Natio…

works_created.t: Que reste-t-il de nos amours? (I Wish You Love)

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: "La Mer," yeah, the old Charles Trenet tune, which was also the thing Bobby Darin made a hit on as “Beyond the Sea,” back in the 50s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
